raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Gas prices in California could increase up to 89.8 cents per gallon in 2025 according to a study by the Marshall School of Business at the University of Southern California.",
    "The California Air Resources Board’s new regulation on the Low Carbon Fuel Standard could contribute 65 cents per gallon to the increase in gas prices.",
    "Governor Newsom's AB2x-1 could cause an additional increase in gas prices of 4.7 cents to 27 cents per gallon by requiring oil refiners to maintain a ready stockpile of gasoline.",
    "The closure of the Phillips 66 refinery could reduce California's total refinery capacity by 8.3%, resulting in an increase of 8.2 cents to 14 cents per gallon in gas prices.",
    "California's gasoline excise tax is expected to automatically increase by nearly 2 cents per gallon.",
    "The average Californian driving a gas-powered car would need to earn an additional $600 to $1,000 a year in pre-tax income to 'break even' with 2024 gas prices."
  ]
}
```
